methodsAttempted.query: site:uqu.edu.sa Umm Al-Qura University artificial intelligence policy students exam plagiarism generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official department, initiative, study-plan, and student-directory pages. These were useful for discovery but mostly lacked policy-grade AI-use evidence.

Firecrawl Arabic search located the official Journal of Umm Al-Qura University for Educational and Psychological Sciences AI policy page, plus degree and news pages that were not institutional policy.
Firecrawl search located the official IT and E-Learning Deanship digital-education academic-integrity policy page and several narrower journal or faculty records.
Firecrawl search returned official journal, CV, and course-material records. Course and CV records were not used as university policy evidence.

methodsAttempted.query: uqu.edu.sa official pages for AI, academic integrity, plagiarism, and digital education policies
methodsAttempted.notes: Official pages were fetched with Firecrawl after checking robots.txt by plain HTTP. The robots file did not include path disallow rules for the fetched public URLs.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found one student-facing digital-education academic-integrity page from the IT and E-Learning Deanship and one journal-scope AI policy page from the Journal of Umm Al-Qura University for Educational and Psychological Sciences. Official AI initiative, degree, news, and faculty file-library pages were documented but rejected as training, curriculum, event/news, or unit-specific material rather than policy-grade evidence for broader institutional AI use.

artifacts.verificationNotes: Official faculty file-library page for a departmental academic-integrity guide. It is narrower than the deanship-level digital-education policy page and was not used for university-level claims.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page describes an initiative to develop artificial intelligence and machine learning skills and lists objectives, program features, and joining requirements.
artifacts.notes: Official source, but it is training/program information rather than AI policy or academic-integrity guidance.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The Arabic page lists the artificial intelligence bachelor program, department, college, study plan, admission conditions, and course sequence.
artifacts.notes: Official curriculum page. It mentions AI coursework but does not state AI-use policy or rules.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The article reports that the university organized the International Artificial Intelligence in Education Competition 2026 and describes participation, projects, workshops, and awards.
artifacts.notes: Official news item only; no policy requirement or guidance was extracted.

artifacts.evidenceSnippet: The page is a College of Social Sciences file-library entry titled دليل النزاهة الأكاديمية and links to a departmental PDF.
artifacts.notes: Not used because the deanship-level digital-education academic-integrity policy was more authoritative and directly readable.

stopConditions: Stop after verified official source snapshots support narrow claim candidates.
Reject official pages that are only curriculum, event/news, research showcase, or department-only material.
Do not use login-gated content or raw PDFs as staged Git artifacts.

artifacts.claimText: Umm Al-Qura University's IT and E-Learning Deanship defines academic integrity in digital education and identifies cheating, plagiarism, improper collaboration, impersonation, assistance, and falsification as forms of academic dishonesty; it says students are expected to recognize plagiarism and ensure submitted assessments are their own work.

citation.citationTitle: سياسات النزاهة الأكاديمية في التعليم الرقمي - عمادة تقنية المعلومات والتعليم الإلكتروني - وكالة الجامعة للشؤون الأكاديمية | جامعة أم القرى
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: Umm Al-Qura University journals state that AI tools, including ChatGPT, GPT-4, Google Bard, Claude, and other large language models, cannot be listed as authors in submissions; human authors must disclose any AI use in creating article content.

citation.citationTitle: سياسة الذكاء الاصطناعي - مجلة جامعة أم القرى للعلوم التربوية والنفسية - ادارة الجمعيات العلمية | جامعة أم القرى
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: For Umm Al-Qura University journals, reviewers are told not to use AI tools to generate, summarize, or draft confidential peer-review reports, and to avoid uploading confidential research content to third-party AI tools.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: هناك عدة أنواع من الأفعال التي تعتبر مسيئة لمعايير الصدق الأكاديمي؛ من بينها الغش، والانتحال، والتعاون غير اللائق، وانتحال الهوية، والمساعدة، والتزييف والتزوير. ... من المتوقع أن يتعرف الطلاب على الانتحال وطرق تجنبه، لذلك يجب على الطالب التأكد من أن جميع التقييمات المقدمة من قبله هي من مجهوده الخاص.
artifacts.evidenceSnippetDisplay: The page lists cheating, plagiarism, improper collaboration, impersonation, assistance, falsification, and forgery as academic dishonesty, and says students should ensure submitted assessments are their own work.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Page section: سياسات التعليم الرقمي / سياسات النزاهة الأكاديمية في التعليم الرقمي
artifacts.rightsNote: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: لا يمكن تسجيل أدوات الذكاء الاصطناعي (بما في ذلك Chat GPT, GPT-4, Google Bard, Claude ونماذج اللغات الكبيرة الأخرى) كمؤلفين في أي تقديم إلى مجلات جامعة أم القرى. ... يجب تسجيل المؤلفين البشريين فقط الذين يستوفون معايير التأليف، ويجب الإفصاح عن أي استخدام للذكاء الاصطناعي في صناعة محتوى الورقة العلمية.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow academic-integrity claim for digital education. The claim does not state AI-specific rules.
Approved as journal-scope AI publication guidance only; it should not be read as a university-wide student AI policy.
Approved as journal-scope reviewer confidentiality guidance. Evidence directly mentions confidential peer-review reports and third-party AI tools.

artifacts.title: Umm Al-Qura University staged AI and academic integrity source review
artifacts.summary: Official-source review for Umm Al-Qura University found a deanship-level digital-education academic-integrity policy and a journal-scope AI policy for Umm Al-Qura University journals. Claims were intentionally scoped to those sources. Official AI initiative, degree, competition-news, and faculty file-library pages were rejected because they did not provide policy-grade AI-use evidence.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The AI publication claims are scoped to Umm Al-Qura University journals and do not establish an institution-wide student AI-use policy.
No central official student-facing generative AI policy was located in the checked official sources.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
